name: SA Conveyancing Clauses
description: Clauses for South African conveyancing transactions, covering voetstoots, occupation, suspensive conditions, FICA, transfer duty, sectional title, municipal rates, breach, and jurisdiction

clauses.title: Voetstoots (As Is)
clauses.description: Seller sells in current condition; buyer accepts latent defects subject to CPA consumer-buyer carve-outs

content.text: The property is sold voetstoots, meaning that
accepts the property in its current condition, including any patent and latent defects, and
shall not be liable for any defects, whether disclosed or undisclosed, save as expressly required by law.
Where the Consumer Protection Act 68 of 2008 applies to this transaction, the rights of

as a consumer shall not be excluded or limited by the voetstoots provisions above. In particular, section 55 of the Consumer Protection Act protects a consumer-buyer from a supplier-seller in the ordinary course of business, and those statutory rights remain unaffected.

clauses.title: Occupation Date & Occupational Rent
clauses.description: Date buyer takes occupation; occupational rent per month if occupation does not coincide with registration date

content.text: Occupation of the property shall be given to
on the occupation date stipulated in the offer to purchase, which date may precede or follow the date of registration of transfer.
Should occupation be given prior to registration,

shall pay occupational rent to the seller, in advance, from the occupation date until the date of registration of transfer. Should occupation be given after registration, the seller shall pay occupational rent to
for the period between registration and occupation. Occupational rent shall be calculated on a monthly basis and apportioned pro-rata for part months.

clauses.title: Suspensive Condition -- Bond Approval
clauses.description: Sale subject to buyer obtaining mortgage bond approval within N business days; failure voids the sale

content.text: This sale is subject to the suspensive condition that
obtains approval in principle for a mortgage bond, from a registered financial institution, in the amount and on terms acceptable to
, within twenty-one (21) business days of the signature date of the offer to purchase.

If bond approval is not obtained within the stipulated period (as it may be extended in writing by agreement between the parties), this agreement shall lapse and be of no further force or effect, and any deposit paid shall be refunded to
without interest or deduction, save for any bank charges actually incurred.

clauses.title: Transfer Duty Liability
clauses.description: Purchaser liable for transfer duty per Transfer Duty Act 40 of 1949 unless the transaction is subject to VAT

content.text: shall be liable for the payment of transfer duty to the South African Revenue Service in accordance with the Transfer Duty Act 40 of 1949, calculated on the purchase price of the property.

Where the seller is a registered value-added tax vendor and the sale constitutes a taxable supply, value-added tax shall be payable in lieu of transfer duty, and the purchase price shall be deemed to include value-added tax at the prevailing statutory rate unless expressly stated otherwise.

clauses.title: FICA Compliance
clauses.description: Both parties to provide FICA documentation per Financial Intelligence Centre Act 38 of 2001

content.text: is an accountable institution as contemplated in the Financial Intelligence Centre Act 38 of 2001 ("FICA"). Each party undertakes to provide
with all documents and information reasonably required to enable
to comply with its customer due diligence obligations under FICA.

Failure by any party to provide the required FICA documentation timeously may delay the registration of transfer, and the defaulting party shall be liable for any holding costs, additional disbursements, or penalties arising from such delay.

clauses.title: Sectional Title Levies
clauses.description: Monthly levies apportioned pro-rata to occupation date; special levies allocated to seller if raised pre-transfer

content.text: Where the property is a sectional title unit, all monthly levies payable to the body corporate shall be apportioned between the seller and
on a pro-rata basis as at the date of occupation, with the seller liable for levies up to and including the occupation date and
liable for levies from the day following the occupation date.

Any special levy raised by the body corporate in respect of a decision taken by the trustees or members prior to the date of transfer shall be for the seller's account, irrespective of when such special levy becomes payable.

clauses.title: Body Corporate Clearance
clauses.description: Seller to obtain levy clearance certificate from the body corporate per Sectional Titles Schemes Management Act 8 of 2011

content.text: Where the property is a sectional title unit, the seller shall, at the seller's cost, obtain a levy clearance certificate from the body corporate in accordance with section 15B(3)(a)(i)(aa) of the Sectional Titles Act 95 of 1986, as read with the Sectional Titles Schemes Management Act 8 of 2011.
shall not be obliged to lodge the transfer documents at the Deeds Office until such clearance certificate has been issued and delivered to

clauses.title: Municipal Rates Clearance
clauses.description: Seller to obtain municipal rates clearance certificate per Local Government: Municipal Systems Act 32 of 2000 section 118

content.text: The seller shall, at the seller's cost, obtain a rates clearance certificate from the relevant municipality in accordance with section 118 of the Local Government: Municipal Systems Act 32 of 2000, confirming that all amounts due for municipal services and rates for the two years preceding the date of application have been paid in full.
shall not be obliged to lodge the transfer documents at the Deeds Office until a valid rates clearance certificate has been issued.

clauses.title: Breach & Cost of Cancellation
clauses.description: Breach remedies; defaulting party liable for cancellation costs, agent commission, and reinstatement charges

content.text: If any party (the "defaulting party") commits a breach of any material term of this agreement and fails to remedy such breach within seven (7) business days of written notice from the other party (the "aggrieved party") calling upon the defaulting party to do so, the aggrieved party shall be entitled, without prejudice to any other rights at law, to either:
claim specific performance of this agreement, together with damages, if any; or
cancel this agreement, retain any deposit and amounts paid as rouwkoop, and recover such further damages as may be proved.
The defaulting party shall be liable for all costs of cancellation, including any agent's commission payable, wasted disbursements, and reinstatement charges on an attorney-and-own-client basis.

clauses.title: SA Law & Jurisdiction
clauses.description: Agreement governed by South African law; parties submit to jurisdiction of Magistrate's Court per section 45 of the Magistrates' Courts Act 32 of 1944

content.text: This agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of South Africa, and the parties hereby irrevocably submit to the jurisdiction of the South African courts in relation to any dispute arising out of or in connection with this agreement.

Notwithstanding that any amount in dispute may exceed the monetary jurisdiction of the Magistrate's Court, the parties consent to the jurisdiction of the Magistrate's Court having territorial jurisdiction in terms of section 45 of the Magistrates' Courts Act 32 of 1944, at the election of the party instituting proceedings.
